subcodes.first_line: O thou who gazest upon the Countenance! Hear thou My call, and be thou not perturbed by that which hath befallen the loved ones of God and His trusted servants [3.5s]... ...The generations that have gone on before you—whither are they fled? And those round whom in life circled the fairest and the loveliest of the land, where now are they?
subcodes.first_line_orig: یا ایها الناظر الی الوجه اسمع ندائی و لاتضطرب عما ورد علی احباء الله و امنائه
subcodes.subjects: @chastisement and requital, @transience; worthlessness of the physical world, @love of God, @detachment; severance; renunciation; patience, @suffering and imprisonment
